title: Engine Registry
description: Schema for engines.json — declares available chess engines. All paths are relative to the repo root. Adding a new engine requires only a new JSON entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the engine (e.g. 'random-engine'). Used as a key in config and CLI flags.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the UI (e.g. 'Random Engine').

dir.description: Path to the engine directory relative to the repo root (e.g. 'engines/random-engine').

build.description: Shell command to build the engine, run from 'dir'. Use null for pre-built binaries. Examples: 'uv venv .venv && uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python -e .' (Python), 'cmake -B build && cmake --build build' (C++), 'cargo build --release' (Rust).

run.description: Shell command to launch the UCI engine binary, run from 'dir'. Must start a process that speaks the UCI protocol on stdin/stdout (e.g. '.venv/bin/python -m random_engine').
